title: 5.3 and 5.4 practice
description: Practice what you learned in lessons 5.3 and 5.4!
topic_path: Systems Restructure in Europe and China | 5.4

assessment_items.question_content:
**If we set aside the idea that the Middle Ages were "barbaric", what is a more practical reason historians might refer to this period as the "Dark Ages"?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Literacy and written records sharply declined, making the era harder to study.
Most medieval people rejected Christianity and ancient Roman knowledge.
Constant invasions prevented trade with regions outside Europe.
Monarchs intentionally destroyed libraries to keep people uneducated.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best explains how the rise of Christianity is linked to the idea of the “Dark Ages”?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Christian Church emphasized faith over classical learning, which some believe led to a decline in cultural progress.
Christianity encouraged widespread literacy, which led to a revival of ancient knowledge and the end of the Dark Ages.
The Church rejected Roman traditions entirely, causing cultural and political institutions to collapse overnight.
Christianity brought political unity so quickly that there was no room for independent intellectual development.

assessment_items.question_content:
>…for you, if you should long outlive me…there is perhaps a better age in store; this slumber of forgetfulness will not last forever. After the darkness has been dispelled, our grandsons will be able to walk back into the pure radiance of the past.  

>—letter written by Francesco Petrarch


**Based on the passage, what is Petrarch’s perspective on the Middle Ages?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He sees the Middle Ages as a temporary period of darkness before a future revival of classical greatness.
He believes the Middle Ages are the pinnacle of human achievement and will last forever.
He thinks the Middle Ages are worse than the future will be, with no hope for improvement.
He views the Middle Ages as a golden age that should be preserved and celebrated.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the main reason feudalism developed in Europe after the fall of the Western Roman Empire?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Feudalism compensated for the lack of a centralized authority.
Germanic tribes introduced feudalism when they took over most cities.
Feudalism was a result of the exchange networks that flourished during this time.
In the wake of the empire’s fall, feudalism was equally beneficial for everyone.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What evidence is there that Europe’s "Dark Age" was actually a time of progress?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: advancements in agriculture, the rise of trade cities, and the founding of universities
the rediscovery of Greek and Roman texts during the early medieval period
the lack of security and order in Europe without a centralized authority
the development of new languages and improved physical health of Europeans

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was happening in China during Europe’s so-called “Dark Age”?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: China thrived under the Tang and Song Dynasties, with strong leadership, active trade, and major innovations.
China faced similar problems as Europe, with low birth rates, minimal trade, and widespread fear.
The Tang Dynasty struggled, but prosperity returned under the Song, which reopened trade networks.
China expanded its territory dramatically, conquering the Khmer and Abbasid Empires.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which groups were primarily involved in the Crusades, and what was their main goal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Christian and Muslim armies fought for control of Jerusalem and other sacred sites.
European kingdoms fought each other to gain control over trade routes in Asia.
The Byzantine Empire and Mongols allied to defend their borders from invading Christians.
Muslim empires invaded Europe to stop the spread of Christianity.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statements best explain the key differences that developed between Latin Christendom and Byzantine Christendom?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Latin Christians followed the pope and used Latin; Byzantines rejected papal authority and used Greek.
Byzantine Christians emphasized Jesus’s divine nature, while Latin Christians focused more on his humanity.
Latin Christians opposed religious imagery, while Byzantine Christians promoted it.
Byzantine Christians believed in many gods, unlike Latin Christians who were monotheistic.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement most accurately describes the Great Schism?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Christian Church split into Eastern Orthodox and Roman Catholic branches due to political and theological disagreements.
It was a civil war between eastern and western regions of the Roman Empire over control of Constantinople.
It was the moment when Martin Luther broke away from the Catholic Church, starting the Protestant Reformation.
It was the conflict between the Catholic Church and Islamic empires during the Crusades.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What agricultural reforms under the Ming Dynasty led to a major increase in food production?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They redistributed farming land to soldiers and the poor.
They reduced the number of farmers to limit overproduction.
They banned private land ownership to create large state-run farms.
They focused only on trade and imported most of their food.

assessment_items.question_content:
>She raised those of talent regardless of family rank. Her laws were strict, but just. Though she was a woman, she ruled with the strength of a great emperor, bringing peace to the empire and order to the court.  

>—adapted from an official Tang Dynasty document on Empress Wu


**Based on the passage, what perspective does this source reflect about Empress Wu’s rule?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: She defied traditional gender roles by ruling effectively and promoting merit.
She was respected for ruling quietly behind the scenes through her sons.
She relied heavily on the military to gain and keep power.
She followed Confucian tradition by staying out of political affairs.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the achievements of China’s “Golden Age”?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It was a time of remarkable progress in art, technology, government, and trade under the Tang dynasty.
It was a period of widespread military conquest and expansion across Europe and the Middle East.
It was an era when China’s economy collapsed, leading to widespread famine and rebellion.
It was a time when foreign cultures dominated China’s political institutions and traditions.
